response:
# Verdict: REVISE ## Overall Assessment This radio button group has **critical accessibility design gaps** that pass automated tests but fail real screen reader users. The component uses native HTML correctly at an individual element level (labels, inputs) but fails to semantically group related options—a fundamental WCAG violation for form controls. Screen readers will announce each option without context about what they're selecting from or why these options belong together. ## Pre-commitment Predictions Before reading code: I predicted the radio group would lack fieldset/legend semantic grouping, which is confirmed as a CRITICAL finding. Also expected visual-only labeling issues and missing accessible name for the entire control set. --- ## Critical Findings (blocks access) ### 1. Missing Fieldset Element to Group Radio Options ```jsx // src/components/RadioGroup.tsx:6-20
``` **Evidence:** Line 7 uses `
` instead of native `
` for grouping radio buttons **User group impacted:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only navigation context **WCAG/APG citation:** WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; WAI-ARIA APG Radio Button Group pattern **Why this matters:** Without fieldset/legend, screen readers announce each option without announcing the group name or relationship between options. Users cannot understand what category these selections belong to (e.g., "Gender", "Payment Method"). This is a complete access barrier for understanding form context. **Fix:** Replace `
` with `
` and add `` element: ```jsx
{label} {options.map((option) => ( // ... radio options ))}
``` ### 2. Missing Legend Element for Accessible Group Name **Evidence:** Line 8-9 uses `
{label}
` instead of semantic legend **User group impacted:** Screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) **WCAG/APG citation:** WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; WAI-ARIA APG Radio Button Group pattern **Why this matters:** The visual label in a div is not announced by screen readers at all. Users cannot understand what these radio options represent without the legend being programmatically associated with the fieldset. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value for the group as a whole. **Fix:** Move `{label}` into `` element inside fieldset: ```jsx
{label}
``` --- ## Major Findings (significantly degrades experience) ### 3. Visual Label Not Associated with Fieldset **Evidence:** Line 8-9 `
{label}
` outside fieldset scope **User group impacted:** Screen reader users, low vision users relying on visual context **WCAG/APG citation:** WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; WAI-ARIA APG Radio Button Group pattern **Why this matters:** The label is visually present but semantically disconnected from the radio options. Users with screen readers will hear "Gender" (if that's the label) then immediately list of Male/Female/Other without understanding these are gender selection options. This creates confusion about what information they're providing. **Fix:** Remove `
` and place `{label}` inside `` element ### 4. Radio Group Name Not Announced to Assistive Technology ```jsx ``` **Evidence:** Line 13 uses `name="options"` for form submission but not accessible grouping **User group impacted:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only navigation context **WCAG/APG citation:** WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WAI-ARIA APG Radio Button Group pattern **Why this matters:** The `name` attribute groups radios for form submission but does NOT provide an accessible name to screen readers. Screen readers need the `` element (not just a div with text) to announce what these options are about. This is a common anti-pattern where developers think `name="options"` provides grouping semantics—it doesn't from AT perspective. **Fix:** The fieldset/legend combination IS the accessible name mechanism—remove reliance on visual-only label --- ## Minor Findings (friction but workaround exists) ### 5. No aria-describedby for Error States ```jsx // Not shown in code, but if validation errors exist: {option} ``` **Evidence:** If this component integrates with form validation, error messages aren't associated **User group impacted:** Screen reader users during validation states **WCAG/APG citation:** WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; WAI-ARIA APG Form Validation pattern **Why this matters:** If radio options have validation errors (e.g., required field), error messages need `aria-describedby` association to be announced properly with the input ### 6. No aria-invalid State for Invalid Selections ```jsx ``` **Evidence:** Missing ability to mark invalid selections **User group impacted:** Screen reader users during validation states **WCAG/APG citation:** WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification; WAI-ARIA APG Form Validation pattern **Why this matters:** If a radio option is required but not selected, the fieldset needs `aria-invalid="true"` to announce invalid state --- ## What's Missing (gaps and unhandled edge cases) - **No `` element**: The group name must be in legend for screen readers - **No semantic grouping structure**: Fieldset is required by WCAG 1.3.1 for related form controls - **Missing error state handling**: If validation occurs, fieldset needs `aria-invalid` and errors need association via aria-describedby on the first input or a summary element - **No language attribute propagation**: The label text should have proper lang if it contains foreign content (not shown but worth noting) --- ## Multi-Perspective Notes ### Screen reader user: Without fieldset/legend, NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver will announce each radio option without announcing the group name. User hears "Male", then "Female", then "Other" with no context about what category these belong to. This is confusing and violates WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships. ### Keyboard-only user: Tab order works fine (each label/input pair), but keyboard users miss the semantic grouping when navigating via screen reader or assistive navigation tools that rely on fieldset boundaries for context switching between form sections. ### Low vision user: Visual grouping exists, but without legend element in DOM, high contrast mode and magnification don't improve accessibility—screen readers still
